subject: New card MPI-102 (dead Install/Uninstall button after an uninstall) — you had the deepest probe context on this family, please review + add what your probing ruled out
body:
Spun off a new card MPI-102 from the MPI-100 verify session. Symptom: after UNINSTALLING a model, pressing INSTALL on a card does nothing — no download, NO backend log line (dies renderer-side), console clean. Same family as the dead-UNINSTALL button you fixed in MPI-99, but now it's the INSTALL button. The user's read (and mine): the real root is a card that goes unresponsive after an uninstall, and the MpiModal show() re-arm fixed only one face of it.

MY ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (in MPI-102 brief.md — please confirm or correct): a finished install leaves a job in state.downloadJobs with status 'complete' that is never removed. On the post-uninstall renderList in MpiModelManager.js, the uninstalled-card branch guards with `if (downloadState !== 'idle')` (~line 362) — 'complete' satisfies that, so the card wires pause/resume/cancel and SKIPS the `delete` (Install) handler at line 371 → dead button. The installed branch has the mirror risk via `isActiveDownload` (~line 251). Contrast: download:cancelled (line ~469) explicitly awaitReSync()s 'so handlers wire to delete (Install) again' — the uninstall path has no equivalent and never normalizes a stale 'complete'. Proposed fix: (1) treat 'complete'/'cancelled' as 'idle' in renderList's action-choice so the correct primary handler always wires; (2) clear state.downloadJobs for the model before the re-render (mirror cancel).

WHY YOU: you burned a probing pass on this file/family for MPI-99 — your checklist had 'confirm failing link (binding vs .show() no-op) via DevTools probe'. What did that probe actually establish? Specifically: did you ever see whether `card.on(...)` was firing vs not after a re-render, and did you observe stale state.downloadJobs entries? Anything you ruled out would save MPI-102's owner a repeat probe, and your context might confirm/kill my stale-'complete'-job theory outright.

MPI-102 is APP-ONLY, no image rebuild (renderer state). Card: .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-102/ (task.json + brief.md). If you want to TAKE MPI-102 yourself (you know the file best), your call — tell the user. Otherwise please drop your probe findings into MPI-102's brief.md or reply here. Thanks!

recent_events.note: MPI-99 author confirmed the stale-complete-job theory + linked research/mpi99-author-context.md; not taking MPI-102.
